title: acer saccharinum subsp. floridanum
intro: Acer saccharinum subsp. floridanum, also known as the Florida sugar maple, is a subspecies of the sugar maple tree native to the southeastern United States. It possesses medicinal properties due to its content of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, alleviating headaches and joint pain, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the Florida sugar maple can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
